reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own Q1 answer (3 × 18), and historically B and C have never been wrong, while D’s Q4 has been correct in all recent games and often double-checked. With only one capsule per question and no conflicts, the chance this full password is wrong is much smaller than the 1/4 random-death risk if nobody volunteers. Verifying would just cost a coin for very little extra safety, so volunteering with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D is the best move.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (88 - 51) is basic arithmetic and I’m very confident my sealed answer is correct. So far, across many games with similarly simple puzzles, we’ve only ever seen one wrong initial capsule; all recent passwords built from single-endorsed capsules have been correct. That makes the chance that at least one of the four current capsules is wrong much lower than the 25% chance I die if nobody volunteers and a random player is killed.  

In earlier games where I didn’t volunteer in Round 1, others tended to verify or pass rather than step up, which often led to random eliminations. Verifying now would still expose me to that same random-death risk this round while costing me a coin. Volunteering immediately with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival odds and preserves all 4 of my coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m confident my own capsule for Q3 (12−7=5) is correct, and in all recent games everyone’s simple arithmetic has almost always been right—D’s earlier slip on Q4 hasn’t repeated and their later Q4s have been verified multiple times. The main way players have died unnecessarily was when nobody volunteered and the system killed someone at random (which has already happened to me once). If I pass or verify, I risk that same random 1-in-4 elimination whenever everyone hesitates; volunteering instead trades that for the much smaller chance that one of the other three easy questions is wrong. With no conflicting capsules available, I’ll enter the straightforward set: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
